entry.title: ThreatsDay: Android Spyware, PLC Attacks, AI Image Prompt Injection + 12 More Stories
entry.summary: Most of this week's trouble came dressed as something useful. A package stole data. A fake extension opened remote access. A safety app became spyware. An image gave hidden orders to an AI agent. Other threats hid in open systems, weak code, and normal network traffic. The threats change every week. Subscribe, and we’l

entry.title: IBM to acquire HRL Laboratories
entry.summary: Exploring the storied history of HRL Laboratories, from the invention of the laser to silicon spin qubits.

entry.title: ollama v0.32.3 released
entry.summary: What's Changed Fixed model downloads that stall before sending data. Improved integrations: restored Claude Code Channels, fixed Anthropic thinking streams, and made Hermes Desktop respect --force-build . Expanded GPU support with CUDA on Windows ARM64, B200 support through CUDA 12, and lower memory use on Linux CUDA/R

entry.title: Runway launches AI model router as generative media gets crowded
entry.summary: Runway no longer wants to be just another AI model company. It wants to become the infrastructure layer for generative media. On Thursday, the startup launched Runway Media Router through Runway Dev, its developer platform, released earlier this month, that provides API access to a growing roster of third-party image,

entry.title: Built a from-scratch BitNet inference engine in pure C - 1.8x faster than bitnet.cpp on Xeon (36 tok/s), zero dependencies [BitNet & Bonsai CPU testers wanted]
entry.summary: Hey r/LocalLLM , Built Project Zero — a from-scratch CPU-only LLM inference engine in pure C99. It beats bitnet.cpp by 1.8× on the same hardware. We also fully support Qwen Bonsai-27B on CPU, and we are looking for the community's help to get x86 CPU benchmark data on the board for both models. What it is Single binary

entry.title: Magnificent 7 Loses $767 Billion as AI Skeptics Dump Tech Stocks
entry.summary: The Magnificent Seven group of megacap technology stocks is on track for its biggest one-day drop since the tariff tantrum in April 2025 as results from Alphabet Inc. and Tesla Inc. are casting doubt on the durability of the artificial intelligence trade that has powered the stock market for more than three years.

entry.title: SAAG: Structured Agent Assessment and Grounding
entry.summary: arXiv:2607.18245v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Exact-match evaluation of agent-calling obscures qualitatively different failure modes: a model may select the right function yet hallucinate argument values, or satisfy a schema while choosing a agent for the wrong reason. Existing benchmarks collapse these distinctions

entry.title: The US Department of Commerce's BIS is formally investigating whether Chinese companies like Moonshot are accessing advanced US AI chips to train their models (Leo Schwartz/The Information)
entry.summary: Leo Schwartz / The Information : The US Department of Commerce's BIS is formally investigating whether Chinese companies like Moonshot are accessing advanced US AI chips to train their models — This week, Trump administration officials have taken to the airwaves and X to blast Chinese open source AI companies like Moon

entry.title: The lawsuit that could kill all AI transparency laws
entry.summary: Elon Musk's company filed a lawsuit against a California law that could, even if it doesn’t win, upend AI disclosure requirements nationwide

entry.title: [tl;dr sec] #338 - OpenAI and Hugging Face, Accelerating EDR Evasion, Google's Mantis
entry.summary: When models decide to find 0-days instead of solving a benchmark, using LLMs to extract EDR logic, Google's AI security review skills and pipeline

entry.title: EU fines Google $1 billion for search, app store antitrust violations
entry.summary: The European Commission fined Google €890 million ($1 billion) on Thursday after finding the company had violated the European Union's Digital Markets Act (DMA), which ensures fair online competition. [...]

entry.title: Russian Global Webmail Espionage
entry.summary: Unit 42 details a Russian cyberespionage campaign targeting Zimbra webmail servers using JavaScript injection to steal credentials. The post Russian Global Webmail Espionage appeared first on Unit 42 .

entry.title: The Rise And Rise Of Billion-Dollar-Plus Rounds
entry.summary: So far this year, 60% of global startup funding across stages — around $320 billion — went to rounds of $1 billion or more, per Crunchbase data, with such rounds instrumental in pushing global funding for the first half of the year to record levels.

entry.title: Quoting Seth Larson
entry.summary: The Python Package Index (PyPI) now rejects new files being uploaded to releases that are older than 14 days. This restriction was put in place to prevent old and long-stable releases from being poisoned in case publishing tokens or workflows of PyPI projects were compromised. As far as we are aware this has not yet be

entry.title: Ropedia raises $22M to scale data collection for training robots
entry.summary: Ropdeia's HOMIE is a head-mounted device, equipped with a 360º camera view, that captures first-person data for training robots. The post Ropedia raises $22M to scale data collection for training robots appeared first on The Robot Report .

entry.title: Quantum Elements Launches Orbit Error Suppression Tool Through IBM Qiskit Platform
entry.summary: Insider Brief Press release – Quantum Elements, a provider of AI-powered digital twins for quantum computing developers, today announced that Orbit, an advanced quantum error suppression tool, will be available through the IBM Qiskit Functions Catalog. The launch will make Orbit directly accessible to IBM Quantum Netwo

entry.title: Launching Health in ChatGPT
entry.summary: Health in ChatGPT now lets eligible U.S. users securely connect medical records and Apple Health to get more personalized insights and better understand their health.

entry.title: Google CEO Pichai says Gemini's next leap depends on building "much larger base models"
entry.summary: Alphabet has raised its 2026 investment forecast to as much as $205 billion, saying demand continues to outpace spending. Google Cloud grew 82 percent in the second quarter. CEO Sundar Pichai says Google needs a larger base model for its next leap in AI and has kicked off an ambitious Gemini 4 training run. The article

entry.title: Google’s Gemini nears billion-user milestone
entry.summary: Gemini had over 750 million monthly users in February.

entry.title: llama.cpp b10103 released
entry.summary: metal : add f16 type support to leaky relu ( #25981 )

entry.title: model: align Laguna with upstream llama.cpp (#17335)
entry.summary: Update llama.cpp to pick up upstream Laguna implementation and remove Ollama's local Laguna implementation. Retain a narrow Metal-only scaling workaround for routed-MoE prompt overflow. Translate older Ollama GGUF attention-gate and SWA metadata names so existing models continue to load.

entry.title: One tampered ChatGPT link could spawn a rogue AI agent that took orders from an attacker every five minutes
entry.summary: Zenity Labs uncovered "AgentForger," a vulnerability in OpenAI's Agent Builder that let a single manipulated ChatGPT link create an autonomous agent on an employee's behalf. The agent inherited the victim's identity and access rights, bypassed approval requirements through the malicious prompt, and pulled new instructi

entry.title: Chocolate Maker Lindt Faces US Lawsuit Over Child Labor Allegations
entry.summary: Lindt & Spruengli AG has been sued by an activist law firm in the US which alleges the Swiss chocolate maker is falsely promoting its efforts to battle child labor in Ghana and the Ivory Coast from where it sources cocoa.

entry.title: Heat Worries US Wheat Farmers Just as Crop Prices Are Surging
entry.summary: For farmers in North Dakota surveying their wheat as harvest closes in, scorching heat is threatening yields just as crop prices rise to a three-year high.

entry.title: Calibrated Selective Fact-Checking via Evidence Chain Evaluation
entry.summary: arXiv:2607.18240v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) can achieve strong fact-checking accuracy, yet forced binary decisions conceal a critical reliability problem: systems may issue confident verdicts even when supporting evidence is weak, sparse, or internally inconsistent. We address this issu

entry.title: GeForce NOW Sets Sail With ‘Path of Exile: Curse of the Allflame’ Joining the Cloud
entry.summary: Lock in and load up the cloud. GFN Thursday brings fresh updates and new adventures, all ready to play without waiting for downloads. Set sail in Path of Exile: Curse of the Allflame and charge in Battlefield 6 Season 4 both launching major content for members this week. Then revisit Capcom legends like Breath of […]

entry.title: AMD’s Instinct MI455X: Aiming for the Sun
entry.summary: Hello you fine Internet folks, here at AMD’s Advancing AI event we are looking at AMD’s brand new Instinct MI455X, replacing the older Instinct MI355X at the top of their AI stack.

entry.title: Poolside's Laguna S 2.1 is a small open-weight coding model that punches well above its size
entry.summary: Poolside has released Laguna S 2.1, its third coding model in three months. Rather than rely on raw scale, the company trained it to keep checking its work, revise failed approaches, and avoid giving up too soon during long agentic sessions. The compact model beats several much larger rivals in benchmarks. Poolside say

entry.title: Quoting Thomas Ptacek
entry.summary: I genuinely believe that if you took an open weights model from 2025 and built a pentest harness for it, it could do this kind of sandbox escape and scan/hack in most networks. This is only surprising because you assume OpenAI has sounder sandboxes. — Thomas Ptacek , doesn't think this even needs a frontier model Tags:

entry.title: The OpenAI/Huggingface incident | Redwood Research podcast episode 2
entry.summary: What are the broader lessons from this incident?
